description_long: This object is a tall, slender utility pole with two vertical posts supporting a pair of parallel wires stretched horizontally between them. The poles are cylindrical and appear to be made of metal, with a dark finish. The wires are thin and taut, running the length between the two posts, and are likely used for electrical or telecommunication purposes. There are no visible transformers, streetlights, or identification tags attached to the structure. The crossarm is a single, horizontal bar connecting the two posts at the top, providing support for the wires. The overall design is minimalistic and functional, typical for urban or suburban power distribution.
description: A tall, dark metal utility pole with two posts and two parallel wires stretched horizontally between them, featuring a single horizontal crossarm at the top.
description_view_0: Side view showing both vertical posts and the two parallel wires clearly stretched between them.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the narrow profile of the pole and wires, with the crossarm visible as a short horizontal bar.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying both posts and wires from the other direction.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite end, again showing the narrow profile and the crossarm at the top.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: A single horizontal crossarm is mounted at the top, connecting the two vertical posts.
